name: research-storage
description: Research file storage conventions and templates for dokhak agents. Use when: (1) saving research results from research-collector or researcher agents, (2) reading cached research files, (3) checking if research exists for a section. Provides directory structure, file format templates, and naming conventions.
capabilities: Define research directory structure for .research/ folder
Create standardized research.md, summary.md, and sources.md templates
Resolve research directories using multi-tier search algorithm
Normalize chapter and section names for consistency
author: BitYoungjae
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation with no executable code. The static scanner flagged 117 issues, but all are false positives: markdown code fences were misidentified as shell backticks, metadata strings as weak crypto, and YAML frontmatter as obfuscation. The skill only describes conventions and uses Read, Write, Glob scoped to .research/ directory.
valueStatement: Research files become scattered and hard to find. This skill provides consistent storage conventions, naming patterns, and templates so research data stays organized and reusable across dokhak sessions.
limitations: Does not perform actual research or web searches
Does not execute code or run external processes
Only provides conventions, requires other skills for research collection
Requires researcher or research-collector agent to create initial files

useCases.title: Organize research cache
useCases.description: Keep research organized across writing sessions so information can be reused and updated efficiently.

useCases.title: Standardize file naming
useCases.description: Apply consistent naming conventions so research files are easy to locate by chapter and section.

useCases.title: Share research templates
useCases.description: Provide standardized templates for research output so all agents produce consistent, reusable research files.

promptTemplates.title: Save new research
promptTemplates.scenario: Store research for a new section
promptTemplates.prompt: Save research for section {chapter}.{section} {title} to .research/sections/{path}/ using the research-storage conventions.

promptTemplates.title: Check existing research
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if research exists for section {chapter}.{section} {title} using the multi-tier resolution strategy.

promptTemplates.title: Read init research
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the init research summary from .research/init/summary.md and sources from .research/init/sources.md.

promptTemplates.title: Apply naming format
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate the canonical directory path for chapter {chapter}, section {section}, title {title} using the buildCanonicalPath function.

bestPractices: Use canonical naming format (zero-padded chapter, no-padded section, kebab-case slug)
Apply the multi-tier resolution strategy when checking for existing research
Follow the provided markdown templates for consistent research file structure
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded paths instead of buildCanonicalPath function
Skipping the multi-tier search when checking for existing research
Storing research outside the .research/ directory structure

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The research-storage skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code using Read, Write, and Glob tools.

faq.question: How many research files can this skill manage?
faq.answer: The skill manages unlimited research files organized by chapter and section within the .research/ directory structure.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with other dokhak skills?
faq.answer: Yes. Research-storage works with researcher, research-collector, writer, and structure-designer agents.

faq.question: Is my research data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads and writes to the .research/ directory. No data leaves your local project.

faq.question: Why is my research not being found?
faq.answer: Use the multi-tier resolution strategy. The skill handles naming variations like 1-2-intro vs 01-2-intro automatically.

faq.question: How is this different from a simple file storage tool?
faq.answer: This skill provides standardized naming, templates, and resolution algorithms specifically designed for research workflows.
